recipient_hypotheses.reality: You personally skim your own inbound in short bursts between meetings. You open maybe a third of cold messages, read a few lines, and reply to almost none — but a genuinely interesting, immediately-legible note from a real person sometimes gets a one-line answer.

An assistant screens everything. Cold messages reach you only if the assistant flags them as clearly relevant and credible; the assistant forwards perhaps one in fifty, with a one-line summary. You see nothing else.

In practice you only engage inbound that arrives through people you trust — a portfolio founder, a partner, a friend forwarding with a vouch. Direct cold contact, however good, effectively never gets a response from you.

You ignore claims and read artifacts. A message pointing at something concrete and checkable (a memo, a result, a running system) occasionally earns a short reply asking for the artifact; rhetoric alone never does.
You simply do not respond to unsolicited contact from strangers, ever. The only path to your attention is through your existing network.

p_operator_agrees_intro.basis: operators in read-only shadow mode know the work; an intro request is a real but plausible favor

p_operator_agrees_forward.basis: forwarding a memo is a smaller favor than a vouched intro

ff_partner_passthrough_discount.basis: value realized only if the partner routes it onward or engages themselves

p_pilot_confirms.basis: shadow-mode replay results transferring to a live operator pilot
